page: Amazon Web Services c7a.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor and an x86_64 AMD EPYC 9R14 processor. It features 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 96.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage and baseline network bandwidth of 19 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance, particularly in CPU-bound and memory-bound operations. The c7a.12xlarge achieves top 10% placement in stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests, as well as Geekbench multi-core and software compilation benchmarks. Memory performance is strong, with DDR5 memory bandwidth reaching top-tier status for small-block read operations and strong status for large-block read and write operations. Cryptographic and vector processing capabilities are enhanced by hardware support for AVX-512 and SHA extensions.

The primary tradeoff of the c7a.12xlarge is its compute-centric design, which limits memory allocation to 2.0 GB per core and lacks local NVMe storage. This makes it highly cost-efficient for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints or high-speed local disk I/O. It is well-suited for applications such as software compilation, static web serving, Redis key-value stores, and small-to-medium LLM inference tasks. However, performance drops to average levels for large-scale LLM inference workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services c7a.12xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 48 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. Equipped with 96.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory at 5 GHz, it provides a 2.0 GB per core memory ratio. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, relying on a 19 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results place the c7a.12xlarge in the top 10% for multi-core CPU performance, software compilation, and Redis operations, alongside strong memory bandwidth. These specifications make it highly efficient for compute-intensive workloads such as software development, web serving, and small-to-medium model inference, though the lack of local storage and moderate memory-to-core ratio represent key architectural tradeoffs.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7a.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 48 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 96 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 19 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier CPU and software compilation performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7a.12xlarge features 48 dedicated AMD EPYC cores and 96 GB DDR5 RAM, optimized for high-performance compute workloads.
tagline: A dedicated 48-core AMD EPYC compute instance with DDR5 memory, optimized for CPU-intensive processing, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving.
bullet_points: Powered by a 48-core AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz
Equipped with 96.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 5 GHz (2.0 GB per core)
Delivers top-tier multi-core CPU and software compilation benchmark performance
Features 19 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPUs
Optimized for compute-heavy workloads, Redis databases, and web serving
